message:
test(ft-ul4vi): libfuzzer harness for jsonschema validator + compiler

Adds fuzz/fuzz_targets/jsonschema_validator.rs targeting the jsonschema
0.21 crate that ft-5ikbd, ft-2sumi, ft-b35tw, and ft-zaqi8 all use at
the conformance-gate boundary. JSON Schema is a known parser-confusion
class — `{$ref: "#"}` recursion stack-overflow, `oneOf` of N variants
OOM, `pattern: "(a+)+"` regex pinning — and the validator becomes
parser-on-untrusted-input the moment a conformance gate validates an
envelope from an external robot/MCP client.

## Files
- fuzz/fuzz_targets/jsonschema_validator.rs (Archetype 5 + 1):
  three Arbitrary-driven modes
  * Compile(&[u8]) — fuzz the schema compiler with arbitrary bytes
    (untrusted schema path)
  * Validate(&[u8]) — fuzz the validator with a trusted pre-compiled
    schema (wa-robot-envelope.json embedded via include_bytes!) +
    arbitrary data (untrusted data path; today's production model)
  * BothPaths { schema, data } — cross-product mode, both halves
    fuzzer-controlled (the production model when external clients
    supply both schema and data)
  Trusted schema cached in OnceLock so Validate / BothPaths don't
  pay setup cost per iteration. Inputs capped at the bead's
  documented bounds (64 KiB schema, 256 KiB data) so libFuzzer
  doesn't waste cycles on inputs the production code would reject
  at framing.
- fuzz/Cargo.toml: registers `jsonschema_validator` bin and adds
  `jsonschema = { version = "0.21", default-features = false }`
  matching the version pinned in crates/frankenterm-core dev-deps
  so the harness exercises the same crate version as production.
- fuzz/corpus/jsonschema_validator/: 9 seed files
  * 4 real schemas: wa-robot-envelope, wa-robot-accounts,
    ft-config, ft-pattern-pack (compile-path warmup)
  * 4 adversarial: self-referential ($ref: "#"), deep oneOf (50
    variants), regex backtrack pattern ((a+)+), 20-deep allOf
    nesting
  * 1 valid envelope (validate-path warmup)

## Contract pinned

No panic on any byte sequence. compile() and validate() must
return Ok or Err for every input. Stack overflow on $ref recursion,
OOM on oneOf state-machine construction, and regex pinning on
pattern compilation are exactly the bugs this harness hunts.

## Verified
- cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin jsonschema_validator
  → exit 0 in 1m24s (full first build); incremental builds are
  sub-second.

Long-running campaign (cargo +nightly fuzz run jsonschema_validator
-- -runs=5000 or -max_total_time=600) is the deployment step;
harness compiles clean and is registered.

Pattern reuse: matches ft-h8v8v (wire_envelope) and ft-hfbsp
(simd_scan) — Archetype 5 structure-aware Arbitrary input + Archetype
1 crash detector with "no panic on any byte sequence" as the primary
oracle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
